name: LJ’s Chili Con Carne y Judia
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Crushed chilis, more or less
recipeInstructions:
If plantains are available, do not use the potato or banana...slice the plantain into 1/4 inch pieces.
1. Cut meat into 1-inch chunks or thereabouts. Let meat soak in a bowl with the tomatoes and juice, lemon juice, mushrooms, black pepper, crushed chilis, diced peppers, and onions overnight. Remove the meat and brown it in a skillet. This may take some time but is worth it.
2. In a large pot, combine all ingredients except the beans (including the above marinade) and slow cook for three hours.
3. Stir in the beans and cook slowly for an additional hour. Do not cook with the lid on the pot as the chili will not thicken.
4. If the chili is not thick enough, you can add flour mixed with water to achieve the desired consistency.
5. Return the "soupy" ring around the pot and stir back into the simmering chili.
6. Adjust the heat level according to taste preferences. Avoid adding hot sauces that may make the chili too "superficial."
